message:
test: lock two boundary invariants — UTF-8-aware log truncation and FtviWriter::finish at non-zero stream offset

Two targeted test additions. Both are regression tests for
already-correct code paths; both catch a narrow class of future
refactors that would silently break.

1. `truncate_for_log_exact_utf8_boundary_preserves_budgeted_prefix`
   in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/wait_execution.rs`
   -----------------------------------------------------------------
   Why: `truncate_for_log("привет", 7)` should return `"пр..."`.
   "пр" is two Cyrillic chars, 4 bytes; with the ellipsis taking 3
   bytes the budget lands *exactly* on a UTF-8 boundary. The
   existing code walks char boundaries and should keep "пр", but
   an "off-by-one" refactor that backs off one extra char to be
   "safe" would silently shorten log lines on boundary cases, and
   the existing tests (which only cover strictly-shorter-than-
   budget and way-over-budget cases) would not catch it.

   The test asserts exact equality on the boundary case so any
   such refactor fails fast.

2. `writer_finish_restores_end_position_after_nonzero_offset`
   in `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/vector_index_tests.rs`
   -----------------------------------------------------------
   Why: `FtviWriter::new` remembers a starting offset so the
   index can be appended to a `Cursor` that already holds prior
   bytes (the common case: appending an FTVI index to an existing
   scrollback container). `FtviWriter::finish` then patches the
   `count` field at `start + 8` and *must* leave the stream
   positioned at the end of the written index, not at the patch
   site. A refactor that uses `seek(SeekFrom::Start(start + 8))`
   without a matching seek-back at the end of `finish` would
   cause subsequent writes to overwrite the on-disk index rather
   than extend the file.

   Test setup: pre-writes "prefix" into a cursor, positions at
   offset 6, creates a writer, pushes one 2-dim vector, calls
   `finish`, then writes "tail". Asserts:
   * `count` byte at `prefix_len + 8` == 1 (confirms the patch
     landed at the right offset relative to `start`).
   * After `finish`, the tail bytes are appended *after* the
     index (not overlapping the count-patch location), by reading
     from `cursor.position()` through end of buffer and checking
     `== b"tail"`.

Scope
-----
Test-only additions. No production code touched in either file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
